response.justification: While the candidate brings valuable geospatial expertise through GISCollective and Green Map work, the core technical requirement mismatch is disqualifying. C++ experience ended 11 years ago at National Instruments; current work is TypeScript/Node.js. Zero Rust experience. For a mid-senior individual contributor on critical routing infrastructure requiring modern systems programming, the gap between web/full-stack development and modern C++/Rust systems engineering is too large, and the role explicitly needs someone immediately productive in the primary language.
strengths: Strong geospatial domain expertise from founding GISCollective and building Green Map GIS application
Demonstrated experience with high-scale distributed systems handling millions of requests/second at Dunnhumby
Test-driven development advocate with proven track record creating testing frameworks and teaching testing practices across organizations
concerns: C++ expertise is severely outdated (last used ~11 years ago); recent 8+ years of work is TypeScript/Node.js, not systems programming
Zero Rust experience despite it being listed as acceptable alternative to primary C++ requirement
GIS background is in data collection/mapping platforms, not routing systems or navigation data pipeline optimization

key_factors.factor: Distributed systems and high-performance infrastructure experience
